raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, at approximately XXXX XXXX  I received a strange call from an American Express representative on a blocked line regarding a fraudulent charge from XX/XX/XXXX to XXXX XXXX. I would like to identify the individual I spoke with, as he attempted to harass, interrogate, and coerce me into admitting to information I does not know, as well as a transaction I did not authorize. 

During our conversation, I explained that the charges in question were not on my account and that I have never used XXXX to purchase items with my American Express credit card. The representative insisted that there was a charge on my XXXX account from XX/XX/XXXX, in the amount of {$10.00} to a company called XXXX XXXX. He claimed that my American Express card had been linked to my XXXX account since that date and suggested that someone in my household must have authorized the payment. 

I reiterated that I have never linked my XXXX account with American Express, nor did I authorize or approve any payments to that entity using my American Express card. The charge he referenced for {$10.00} on XX/XX/XXXX, is false ; it was actually paid using a XXXX debit card.
